moa: Clarithromycin binds to 50S ribosomal subunit → inhibits protein synthesis. Active metabolite 14-hydroxyclarithromycin adds synergy.
commonUses: 1. Respiratory infections — CAP, bronchitis, sinusitis, pharyngitis. 2. Skin infections. 3. H. pylori eradication (with PPI + amoxicillin). 4. MAC prophylaxis and treatment. 5. Pertussis.
dose: 250–500 mg orally every 12 hours for 7-14 days. H. pylori: 500 mg every 12 hours. Extended-release: 1 g once daily. IV: 500 mg every 12 hours over 60 minutes.
pedDose: Children: 7.5-15 mg/kg/day divided every 12 hours. Max: 1 g/day.
pedAgeLimit: >6 months.
pedDoseAr: للأطفال: 7.5-15 مجم لكل كيلوجرام يوميًا مقسمة كل 12 ساعة.
lactationNote: Excreted into breast milk; use with caution.
metabolism: Hepatic — CYP3A4. Active metabolite. Half-life: 3-7 hours.
elimination: Renal (20-30% unchanged), biliary.
interactions: Warfarin — increased INR. Statins — myopathy risk (avoid simvastatin/lovastatin). Colchicine — toxicity (contraindicated). Carbamazepine, phenytoin — increased levels. CCBs — hypotension, AKI.
doseAdjustments: Renal impairment: eGFR <30: reduce by 50%.
sideEffects: Common: Diarrhea, nausea, metallic taste (5-10%), headache. Serious: QT prolongation, C. difficile colitis, hepatotoxicity.
contra: Hypersensitivity to macrolides. Concomitant colchicine, tizanidine, ergot derivatives. Severe QT prolongation.

name: doxycycline
moa: Doxycycline binds to 30S ribosomal subunit → blocks aminoacyl-tRNA binding → inhibits bacterial protein synthesis. Active against atypical bacteria, MRSA, and protozoa.
commonUses: 1. Respiratory tract infections — atypical pneumonia (Mycoplasma, Chlamydia, Legionella). 2. Skin infections — acne, MRSA, cellulitis. 3. STIs — chlamydia, syphilis (alternative). 4. Tick-borne illnesses — Lyme disease, ehrlichiosis, Rocky Mountain spotted fever. 5. Malaria prophylaxis and treatment.
dose: 100–200 mg orally or IV once daily or divided every 12 hours. Acne: 50–100 mg once daily. Malaria prophylaxis: 100 mg once daily.
pedDose: Children >8 years: 2–4 mg/kg/day once daily or divided every 12 hours. Max: 200 mg/day.
pedAgeLimit: >8 years routinely. <8 years only for life-threatening infections.
pedDoseAr: للأطفال أكبر من 8 سنوات: 2–4 مجم لكل كيلوجرام يوميًا. الحد الأقصى 200 مجم يوميًا.
lactationNote: Excreted into breast milk; avoid.
metabolism: Hepatic — minimal. Half-life: 15–25 hours.
elimination: Renal (40% unchanged), biliary (30%).
interactions: Antacids, calcium, iron — reduced absorption (separate by 2-4 hours). Warfarin — increased INR.
doseAdjustments: Renal impairment: no adjustment. Hepatic impairment: caution in severe impairment.
sideEffects: Common: GI upset, photosensitivity (severe), esophagitis. Serious: Intracranial hypertension, hepatotoxicity, tooth discoloration in children.
contra: Pregnancy. Children <8 years (except life-threatening). Hypersensitivity to tetracyclines.

name: clindamycin
moa: Clindamycin binds to 50S ribosomal subunit → inhibits protein synthesis. Excellent anaerobic coverage and active against MRSA. No gram-negative activity.
commonUses: 1. Skin and soft tissue infections — MRSA, cellulitis, abscesses. 2. Aspiration pneumonia, lung abscess. 3. Bone and joint infections. 4. Intra-abdominal infections (with gram-negative coverage). 5. Dental infections (penicillin allergy). 6. Bacterial vaginosis (topical).
dose: 150–450 mg orally every 6–8 hours. Max: 1.8 g/day. IV: 600–900 mg every 8 hours over 10-60 minutes.
pedDose: 10–30 mg/kg/day orally divided every 6–8 hours. Max: 1.8 g/day.
pedAgeLimit: Safe for all ages.
pedDoseAr: 10–30 مجم لكل كيلوجرام يوميًا — كل 6–8 ساعات. الحد الأقصى 1.8 جرام يوميًا.
lactationNote: Excreted into breast milk; compatible.
metabolism: Hepatic — CYP3A4. Half-life: 2–4 hours.
elimination: Biliary/fecal (50-60% as metabolites).
interactions: Neuromuscular blockers — enhanced blockade. Erythromycin — antagonism (avoid). Warfarin — increased INR.
doseAdjustments: Renal: no adjustment. Hepatic: severe — reduce dose by 25-50%.
sideEffects: Common: Diarrhea (10-20%), metallic taste (IV). Serious: C. difficile colitis (highest risk of any antibiotic).
contra: Hypersensitivity. History of C. difficile colitis or IBD (relative).

name: fusidic acid
moa: Fusidic acid inhibits protein synthesis by blocking elongation factor G (EF-G). Active only against staphylococci including MRSA.
commonUses: 1. Skin infections — impetigo, cellulitis (topical). 2. Staphylococcal infections including MRSA (systemic, with other agents). 3. Bone and joint infections. 4. Endocarditis (combination).
dose: Topical: apply thin film 2-4 times daily. Systemic: 500 mg orally every 8 hours. IV: 500 mg every 8 hours over 2 hours.
pedDose: Systemic: 15–20 mg/kg/day divided every 8 hours. Max: 1.5 g/day.
pedAgeLimit: Safe for all ages (systemic use caution in neonates).
pedDoseAr: نظاميًا: 15–20 مجم لكل كيلوجرام يوميًا كل 8 ساعات. موضعيًا: طبقة رقيقة 2–4 مرات يوميًا.
lactationNote: Excreted into breast milk (systemic). Topical likely safe.
metabolism: Hepatic — CYP3A4. Half-life: 10–15 hours.
elimination: Biliary/fecal (90% as metabolites).
interactions: Statins — myopathy/rhabdomyolysis risk (avoid combination). Warfarin — increased INR.
doseAdjustments: Renal: no adjustment. Hepatic: severe — caution.
sideEffects: Topical: local irritation. Systemic: nausea, elevated LFTs (reversible), cholestatic jaundice (rare).
contra: Hypersensitivity. Severe hepatic impairment.

name: linezolid
moa: Linezolid binds to 23S portion of 50S ribosomal subunit → inhibits initiation complex formation. Active against all gram-positives including MRSA, VRE.
commonUses: 1. MRSA infections — pneumonia, SSTI, bacteremia. 2. VRE infections. 3. Nosocomial pneumonia. 4. Multi-drug resistant tuberculosis.
dose: 600 mg orally or IV every 12 hours. IV infusion over 30-120 minutes.
pedDose: Neonates: 10 mg/kg every 12 hours. Children 1 month–11 years: 10 mg/kg every 8 hours. Adolescents: 600 mg every 12 hours.
pedAgeLimit: Safe for neonates.
pedDoseAr: للولدان: 10 مجم لكل كيلوجرام كل 12 ساعة. للأطفال: 10 مجم/كجم كل 8 ساعات.
lactationNote: Excreted into breast milk; avoid.
metabolism: Hepatic — non-enzymatic. Half-life: 4-6 hours.
elimination: Renal (30% unchanged, 50% as metabolites).
interactions: MAO inhibitors — hypertensive crisis (avoid). SSRIs — serotonin syndrome. Tyramine-rich foods — avoid.
doseAdjustments: No adjustments needed.
sideEffects: Common: Diarrhea, nausea, headache, thrombocytopenia (reversible). Serious: Myelosuppression, peripheral neuropathy, optic neuropathy, serotonin syndrome.
contra: MAO inhibitors within 2 weeks. Uncontrolled hypertension, pheochromocytoma, thyrotoxicosis.

name: ciprofloxacin + tinidazole
moa: Ciprofloxacin kills aerobic gram-negatives. Tinidazole kills anaerobes and protozoa. Combined coverage of aerobes + anaerobes.
commonUses: 1. Mixed aerobic-anaerobic infections — intra-abdominal, pelvic inflammatory disease, diabetic foot. 2. Bacterial diarrhea. 3. Dental abscesses.
dose: 500/500 mg orally every 12 hours for 5-14 days. Take 1 hour before or 2 hours after meals.
pedDose: Not recommended for children.
pedAgeLimit: >18 years.
pedDoseAr: غير معتمد للأطفال.
lactationNote: Both drugs excreted into breast milk; avoid.
metabolism: Ciprofloxacin: hepatic 15%. Tinidazole: hepatic. Half-life: cipro 4h, tinidazole 12-14h.
elimination: Ciprofloxacin: renal. Tinidazole: renal and fecal.
interactions: Antacids — reduced absorption (separate). Warfarin — increased INR. Alcohol — disulfiram reaction (avoid).
doseAdjustments: Renal impairment: eGFR <30: ciprofloxacin adjust (24h interval).
sideEffects: Common: Nausea, metallic taste (tinidazole), diarrhea, headache. Serious: Tendinopathy, peripheral neuropathy, QT prolongation, disulfiram reaction.
contra: Hypersensitivity. Children <18 years. Disulfiram use.

name: tobramycin + dexamethasone
moa: Tobramycin is an aminoglycoside that kills bacteria by binding 30S ribosomal subunit → misreading of mRNA. Dexamethasone suppresses inflammation by inhibiting phospholipase A2 → reduces prostaglandin synthesis.
commonUses: 1. Bacterial conjunctivitis with inflammation. 2. Blepharitis. 3. Post-operative ocular inflammation and infection prophylaxis. 4. Keratitis with inflammatory component.
dose: 1-2 drops every 4-8 hours (frequency depends on severity). Reduce to 1-2 drops twice daily once improvement occurs.
pedDose: Same as adult (safe for children >1 year under supervision).
pedAgeLimit: >1 year.
pedDoseAr: نقطة–نقطتين كل 4–8 ساعات حسب الشدة.
lactationNote: Minimal systemic absorption; compatible.
metabolism: Negligible systemic absorption.
elimination: Local effect only.
interactions: No clinically significant interactions (topical).
doseAdjustments: No adjustments needed.
sideEffects: Common: Stinging, burning, blurred vision, conjunctival irritation. Serious: Corneal epithelial toxicity (prolonged use), intraocular pressure elevation (steroid component, with prolonged use), cataract formation.
contra: Hypersensitivity. Viral (HSV, VZV, vaccinia), fungal, or mycobacterial eye infections. Glaucoma (relative contraindication). Corneal epithelial defect.

name: tobramycin
moa: Tobramycin binds to 30S ribosomal subunit → causes mRNA misreading → production of non-functional proteins → bacterial cell death. Active against gram-negative bacteria (Pseudomonas aeruginosa, E. coli, Proteus, Klebsiella) and some gram-positives (Staphylococcus).
commonUses: 1. Bacterial conjunctivitis. 2. Blepharitis. 3. Keratitis. 4. Dacryocystitis. 5. Pre- and post-operative prophylaxis.
dose: 1-2 drops every 4 hours (mild infection) or every 1-2 hours (severe infection). Ointment: 0.5-inch strip 2-3 times daily.
pedDose: Same as adult. Safe for children >1 year.
pedAgeLimit: >1 year.
pedDoseAr: نقطة–نقطتين كل 4 ساعات (عدوى خفيفة) أو كل 1-2 ساعات (شديدة).
lactationNote: Minimal systemic absorption; compatible.
metabolism: Negligible systemic absorption.
elimination: Local effect only.
interactions: No clinically significant interactions (topical).
doseAdjustments: No adjustments needed.
sideEffects: Common: Stinging, burning, blurred vision, conjunctival irritation, punctate keratitis. Serious: Corneal epithelial toxicity (prolonged use), allergic reactions (rare).
contra: Hypersensitivity to aminoglycosides. Viral or fungal eye infections.

name: trimethoprim + sulfamethoxazole
moa: Sulfamethoxazole inhibits dihydropteroate synthase (folate synthesis). Trimethoprim inhibits dihydrofolate reductase. Sequential blockade causes synergistic bactericidal effect.
commonUses: 1. Urinary tract infections. 2. Pneumocystis jirovecii pneumonia (PCP) — treatment and prophylaxis. 3. Shigellosis. 4. MRSA SSTI. 5. Nocardiosis. 6. Toxoplasmosis (with pyrimethamine).
dose: UTI: 160/800 mg every 12 hours for 3-10 days. PCP treatment: 15-20 mg TMP/kg/day divided every 6-8 hours. PCP prophylaxis: 160/800 mg once daily. MRSA: 320/1600 mg every 12 hours.
pedDose: UTI: 4 mg TMP/kg + 20 mg SMX/kg every 12 hours. PCP treatment: 5 mg TMP/kg every 6 hours (IV).
pedAgeLimit: >2 months (avoid in neonates <4 weeks due to kernicterus risk).
pedDoseAr: لالتهابات المسالك البولية: 4 مجم TMP/كجم + 20 مجم SMX/كجم كل 12 ساعة. تجنب الأطفال أقل من 4 أسابيع.
lactationNote: Excreted into breast milk; avoid in first 6 weeks of infant's life.
metabolism: Hepatic — CYP2C9 (SMX). Half-life: TMP 10h, SMX 10h.
elimination: Renal (50-60% unchanged).
interactions: Warfarin — increased INR. Phenytoin — increased levels. Methotrexate — increased myelosuppression. ACE inhibitors — hyperkalemia. Digoxin — increased digoxin levels.
doseAdjustments: Renal impairment: eGFR 30-50: 50% dose; eGFR <30: avoid.
sideEffects: Common: Rash (3-7%), nausea, diarrhea, hyperkalemia. Serious: Stevens-Johnson syndrome (SJS), hepatotoxicity, blood dyscrasias, nephrotoxicity.
contra: Hypersensitivity to sulfonamides. Severe renal/hepatic impairment. G6PD deficiency. Infants <2 months. Pregnancy near term.

name: gentamicin
moa: Gentamicin binds to 30S ribosomal subunit → causes mRNA misreading → misincorporation of amino acids → production of non-functional proteins → bacterial cell death. Active against aerobic gram-negatives including Pseudomonas.
commonUses: 1. Severe gram-negative infections — sepsis, pneumonia, UTIs. 2. Intra-abdominal infections (with anaerobic coverage). 3. Endocarditis (with ampicillin or vancomycin). 4. Topical eye/skin infections.
dose: Traditional: 1–1.7 mg/kg every 8 hours. Extended-interval: 4–7 mg/kg IV every 24 hours. Topical: apply 3-4 times daily.
pedDose: Traditional: 2–2.5 mg/kg every 8 hours. Extended-interval: 4–7.5 mg/kg every 24 hours.
pedAgeLimit: Safe for all ages (requires dose adjustment for neonates).
pedDoseAr: 2–2.5 مجم لكل كيلوجرام كل 8 ساعات أو 4–7.5 مجم/كجم كل 24 ساعة (جرعة مرة يوميًا).
lactationNote: Minimal excretion; topical likely safe.
metabolism: Not metabolized.
elimination: Renal (90-100% unchanged). Half-life: 2-3 hours.
interactions: Diuretics (furosemide) — increased ototoxicity. NSAIDs — increased nephrotoxicity. Neuromuscular blockers — prolonged blockade.
doseAdjustments: Renal impairment: eGFR <30: reduce dose or extend interval. Monitor trough levels.
sideEffects: Common: Nephrotoxicity (5-25%, reversible), ototoxicity (vestibular > cochlear, permanent). Serious: Neuromuscular blockade.
contra: Hypersensitivity to aminoglycosides. Myasthenia gravis.

name: ceftazidime
moa: Ceftazidime binds to penicillin-binding proteins → inhibits cell wall synthesis. Excellent anti-pseudomonal activity. Weak against gram-positives. No anaerobic activity.
commonUses: 1. Pseudomonas aeruginosa infections — pneumonia, UTIs, bacteremia, malignant otitis externa. 2. Severe gram-negative infections — sepsis, meningitis, febrile neutropenia (with anti-GPC agent). 3. Melioidosis. 4. Cystic fibrosis exacerbations.
dose: 1–2 grams IV/IM every 8 hours (every 12 hours for milder infections). Meningitis: 2 grams every 8 hours. Max: 6 g/day (9 g/day for cystic fibrosis).
pedDose: Children 1 month–12 years: 30–50 mg/kg every 8 hours. Neonates: 30–50 mg/kg every 12 hours (week 1) or every 8 hours (weeks 2-4).
pedAgeLimit: Safe for all ages including neonates.
pedDoseAr: للأطفال: 30–50 مجم لكل كيلوجرام كل 8 ساعات. للولدان حسب العمر.
lactationNote: Low excretion; compatible.
metabolism: Not metabolized.
elimination: Renal (80-90% unchanged). Half-life: 2 hours.
interactions: Probenecid — increases levels. Aminoglycosides — additive nephrotoxicity (monitor).
doseAdjustments: Renal impairment: eGFR 30-50: 1-2 g every 12h; eGFR 10-30: 1-2 g every 24h; eGFR <10: 500 mg-1 g every 24-48h.
sideEffects: Common: Injection site pain, diarrhea, rash, elevated LFTs. Serious: Anaphylaxis, C. difficile colitis.
contra: Hypersensitivity to cephalosporins. Severe penicillin anaphylaxis (cross-reactivity).

name: oxytetracycline
moa: Oxytetracycline binds to 30S ribosomal subunit → inhibits protein synthesis (bacteriostatic). Active against gram-positives, gram-negatives, atypicals, and some protozoa.
commonUses: 1. Topical skin infections — acne vulgaris, rosacea. 2. Ocular infections — blepharitis, conjunctivitis (topical ophthalmic). 3. Systemic (rare) — atypical pneumonia (but doxycycline preferred).
dose: Topical (cream/lotion): apply thin film 1-2 times daily. Ophthalmic: 1 drop 4-6 times daily or ointment 2-4 times daily. Systemic (rare): 250–500 mg orally every 6 hours.
pedDose: Topical/ophthalmic: same as adult. Systemic: children >8 years: 25–50 mg/kg/day divided every 6 hours.
pedAgeLimit: Topical: safe for all ages. Systemic: >8 years.
pedDoseAr: موضعيًا وعينيًا: آمن لجميع الأعمار. نظاميًا: للأطفال أكبر من 8 سنوات فقط.
lactationNote: Topical: likely safe. Systemic: avoid.
metabolism: Hepatic — minimal. Half-life: 6-10 hours.
elimination: Renal (60% unchanged).
interactions: Topical: minimal. Antacids reduce systemic absorption.
doseAdjustments: Topical: no adjustment. Systemic: avoid in severe renal impairment.
sideEffects: Topical: local irritation, photosensitivity. Systemic: GI upset, tooth discoloration in children.
contra: Pregnancy. Children <8 years (systemic). Hypersensitivity.

name: bacitracin
moa: Bacitracin binds to C55-isoprenyl pyrophosphate → blocks peptidoglycan recycling → inhibits cell wall synthesis. Active against gram-positive bacteria. No gram-negative activity.
commonUses: 1. Topical skin infections — minor cuts, scrapes, impetigo. 2. Ocular infections — conjunctivitis, blepharitis (with polymyxin or neomycin). 3. Prophylaxis of minor wounds.
dose: Topical (ointment/cream): apply thin layer 1-3 times daily. Ophthalmic: apply thin strip every 3-4 hours.
pedDose: Same as adult. Safe for all ages (negligible absorption).
pedAgeLimit: Safe for all ages.
pedDoseAr: موضعيًا: طبقة رقيقة 1–3 مرات يوميًا. للعين: طبقة رقيقة كل 3–4 ساعات.
lactationNote: Topical application: negligible absorption.
metabolism: Not metabolized systemically (topical).
elimination: Topical: minimal absorption.
interactions: None clinically significant.
sideEffects: Common: Local irritation, burning, rash. Serious: Anaphylaxis (rare), nephrotoxicity (with extensive application to burns).
contra: Hypersensitivity. Deep wounds or severe burns (increased absorption risk).

name: tinidazole
moa: Tinidazole enters parasite/anaerobe → nitro group reduced → generates toxic radicals → damages DNA and inhibits nucleic acid synthesis → cell death. Longer half-life than metronidazole.
commonUses: 1. Giardiasis. 2. Amebiasis (intestinal and liver abscess). 3. Trichomoniasis. 4. Bacterial vaginosis. 5. Anaerobic bacterial infections.
dose: Giardiasis: 2 g once. Amebiasis: 2 g once daily for 3 days. Trichomoniasis: 2 g once (treat partner). Bacterial vaginosis: 2 g once daily for 2 days OR 1 g once daily for 5 days. Anaerobic infections: 1–2 g once daily for 5-10 days.
pedDose: Giardiasis: 50 mg/kg once (max 2 g). Amebiasis: 50 mg/kg once daily for 3 days.
pedAgeLimit: >3 years (metronidazole preferred for younger).
pedDoseAr: للمثقبية المعوية: 50 مجم لكل كيلوجرام مرة واحدة. للأميبا: 50 مجم/كجم مرة يوميًا لمدة 3 أيام.
lactationNote: Excreted into breast milk; discontinue breastfeeding during and for 3 days after therapy.
metabolism: Hepatic — CYP3A4. Half-life: 12-14 hours.
elimination: Renal (25% unchanged), biliary/fecal (50%).
interactions: Alcohol — disulfiram reaction (avoid during and 3 days after). Warfarin — increased INR.
doseAdjustments: Renal: no adjustment. Hepatic: severe — reduce by 50%.
sideEffects: Common: Metallic taste, nausea, vomiting, headache, dark urine. Serious: Peripheral neuropathy (prolonged use), disulfiram reaction.
contra: Hypersensitivity to nitroimidazoles. First trimester of pregnancy. Breastfeeding.

name: racecadotril + nifuroxazide
moa: Racecadotril inhibits enkephalinase → prevents breakdown of endogenous enkephalins → reduces intestinal hypersecretion without affecting motility. Nifuroxazide is a poorly absorbed antibacterial that disrupts bacterial metabolism in the gut lumen.
commonUses: 1. Acute diarrhea — viral, bacterial, traveler's diarrhea. 2. Secretory diarrhea of various etiologies. 3. Pediatric diarrhea.
dose: Racecadotril 100 mg + nifuroxazide 200 mg orally 3-4 times daily for 3-7 days. Continue until 24 hours after diarrhea stops.
pedDose: Children 2-10 years: weight-based. Infant suspension available. Not to exceed 1.5 mg/kg racecadotril per dose.
pedAgeLimit: >3 months for racecadotril; combined form >1 year typically.
pedDoseAr: للأطفال 2–10 سنوات: حسب الوزن. شراب للأطفال متوفر.
lactationNote: Limited data; caution or avoid.
metabolism: Racecadotril: GI hydrolysis to active thiorphan. Nifuroxazide: not absorbed.
elimination: Racecadotril: renal (90%). Nifuroxazide: fecal (100% unchanged).
interactions: No clinically significant interactions.
doseAdjustments: No adjustments needed.
sideEffects: Common: Vomiting, nausea, abdominal pain (less than loperamide). Serious: Allergic reactions (rare).
contra: Hypersensitivity. Severe renal or hepatic failure.
